name: Community Resources And Housing Development Corporation
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor and education provider serving low-to-moderate income communities across Colorado since 1971, offering free homebuyer education and financial guidance.
description_long:
Community Resources and Housing Development Corporation (CRHDC) was founded in 1971 as Colorado Rural Housing Development Corporation to address severe housing and living conditions faced by migrant and seasonal farm workers. Originally focused exclusively on rural markets, the organization expanded its mission in 2009 to serve both urban and rural communities across Colorado and beyond. Today, CRHDC operates as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit with offices in Westminster and Alamosa, Colorado.

CRHDC's primary services include HUD-approved housing counseling, free homebuyer education workshops, affordable mortgage lending, rental property management (300+ units), mutual self-help home construction programs, and housing development. The organization provides comprehensive financial education on home ownership decisions, promotes responsible lending practices, and offers technical assistance to organizations developing agricultural worker housing. Their lending division focuses on providing healthy mortgage finance options, while their development arm actively constructs affordable housing.

CRHDC distinguishes itself through deep community roots spanning 50+ years, membership in NeighborWorks® America, and a demonstrated track record of tangible outcomes: overseeing construction of 2,000+ self-help homes, providing technical assistance for 500+ farm worker housing units, and managing hundreds of rental units statewide. The organization takes a holistic approach combining counseling, education, lending, and housing development rather than offering single-service solutions. Their free CHFA-sponsored homebuyer education classes are specifically designed for low-to-moderate income households.

CRHDC is genuinely nonprofit and free for counseling services, making it ideal for budget-conscious borrowers. However, the organization operates only in Colorado and adjacent regions, limiting geographic accessibility. Their lending products appear limited compared to national lenders, and specific loan terms, rates, and approval criteria are not detailed on their website. Prospective clients should contact them directly to understand complete service availability and eligibility requirements.
pros: HUD Housing Counseling Agency designation ensures government-backed expertise and credibility
Free CHFA-sponsored homebuyer education workshops (full-day Saturday programs)
NeighborWorks® America membership indicates adherence to national nonprofit housing standards
Demonstrated 50+ year track record with 2,000+ self-help homes constructed and managed
Manages 300+ affordable multi-family rental units across Colorado for ongoing affordability
Multilocation presence with offices in Westminster (Denver Metro) and Alamosa (San Luis Valley)
Holistic approach combining counseling, education, lending, development, and property management
cons: Geographic limitation: Services available only in Colorado and adjacent regions, not nationwide
Limited lending details: Specific loan products, terms, rates, and approval criteria not disclosed on website
Smaller organization: May have less capacity and resources compared to national mortgage lenders
Limited online accessibility: No indication of remote counseling or digital application processes
Self-help housing program appears limited to specific geographic areas (currently Monte Vista, Colorado only)
best_for: Colorado low-to-moderate income homebuyers seeking free HUD-approved counseling and education
First-time homebuyers wanting full-day financial education workshops before applying for mortgages
Rural agricultural workers seeking affordable housing development and financing options
Renters interested in transitioning to affordable homeownership through mutual self-help construction programs
diagnosis: CRHDC is best for Colorado residents seeking free, nonprofit housing counseling and education from a HUD-approved agency with proven community impact. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Colorado and adjacent markets—those outside this region cannot access their services, and even within Colorado, their lending and rental options may be limited compared to national providers.
services: Free CHFA-Sponsored Homebuyer Education Workshops (full-day Saturday programs)
Affordable Mortgage Lending with responsible lending practices
Mutual Self-Help Housing Construction Program (USDA Rural Development partnership)
Affordable Multi-Family Rental Management (300+ units statewide)
Technical Assistance for Agricultural Worker Housing Development
Community Education Events on homeownership and financial stability
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
